mo.note: With a first- or second-person recipient, the verb often takes მო-; the scene is anchored toward the speaker/listener (Ego Space).
mo.citation: Rusudan Asatiani, Dynamic Conceptual Model of the Linguistic Structuring of Space: the Georgian Preverbs, section V (მო- with I/II person recipient and object marking).

mi.note: With a third-person recipient, მი- is typical; orientation is toward the non-participant (Alter Space).
mi.citation: Rusudan Asatiani, Dynamic Conceptual Model of the Linguistic Structuring of Space: the Georgian Preverbs, section V (მი- with III person recipient).

mo.note: Hither direction toward speaker/listener; common perfective path for ‘bring’.

examples.gloss: to bring / deliver (lexeme pair with მოტანა)
